claim: Latent Space is the AI Engineer podcast hosted by swyx and Alessio Fanelli; recent April 2026 episodes cover Shopify's AI rollout, Notion knowledge agents, OpenAI Frontier harness work.
quote: Physical AI that Moves the World (Apr 27); Shopify's AI Phase Transition (Apr 22); Extreme Harness Engineering (Apr 7) … Latent Space: The AI Engineer Podcast

claim: Interconnects is Nathan Lambert's newsletter+podcast covering AI models, training methods, and the trajectory of the open-model ecosystem.
quote: Interconnects … audio essays about the latest developments in AI and interviews with leading scientists in the field, breaking the hype and understanding what's under the hood.

claim: Practical AI is hosted by Dan Whitenack and Chris Benson; recent April 2026 episodes include Agentic Coding economics, Comma AI self-driving, and Edge AI constraints.
quote: April 23, 2026 - 'The mythos of Mythos and Allbirds takes flight to the neocloud'; April 16, 2026 - 'Open Source Self-Driving with Comma AI'; April 9, 2026 - 'Post-Mortem of Anthropic's Claude Code Leak'; April 2, 2026 - 'Agentic Coding and the Economics of Open Source'

claim: Dwarkesh Podcast is a weekly long-form interview show on AI, science, and history hosted by Dwarkesh Patel.
quote: Dwarkesh Podcast … long-form interviews with deeply researched preparation

claim: Machine Learning Street Talk is the highest-rated technical AI podcast on Spotify; host Tim Scarfe has a PhD in machine learning and was previously Principal Engineer at Microsoft and Chief Data Scientist at bp.
quote: Machine Learning Street Talk … the highest-rated technical AI podcast on Spotify. Tim Scarfe has a Ph.D in machine learning … worked as a Principal Engineer at Microsoft and Chief Data Scientist at bp.

claim: The AI Daily Brief is Nathaniel Whittemore's daily AI news analysis show, with 960+ episodes since April 2023 and an April 19, 2026 latest episode.
quote: The AI Daily Brief … hosted by Nathaniel Whittemore … 961 episodes … started on April 4th, 2023 with the latest episode from April 19th, 2026

claim: Hard Fork is a weekly New York Times tech podcast hosted by Casey Newton and Kevin Roose, covering AI, social media, and tech policy.
quote: Hard Fork is a podcast from The New York Times hosted by journalists Kevin Roose and Casey Newton who explore … the rapidly changing world of tech.

claim: The Pragmatic Engineer podcast covers software engineering at Big Tech and startups; recent and upcoming guests include Thuan Pham (ex-Uber CTO, now Faire CTO), Martin Kleppmann, and DHH.
quote: Upcoming guests on The Pragmatic Engineer Podcast include Thuan Pham (Uber's first and longest-serving CTO, now CTO at Faire), Martin Kleppmann (author of Designing Data Intensive Applications), and David Heinemeier Hansson.

claim: The Changelog is the open-source weekly hosted by Adam Stacoviak; March 2026 episodes covered Tailscale's TSIDP/Aperture and GitHub Copilot.
quote: On March 11, 2026, Adam talked with Tailscale co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer David Carney about where Tailscale is headed next: TSIDP, TSNet, multiple tailnets, and Aperture.

claim: Acquired is a 4-hour-per-episode company-history podcast by Ben Gilbert and David Rosenthal; #1 tech podcast on Apple/Spotify with over 1M listeners per episode.
quote: Acquired is the #1 technology podcast on Apple and Spotify, with each episode reaching over 1 million listeners. As of 2024, the podcast has more than 600,000 monthly listeners.

claim: The Cognitive Revolution is a biweekly podcast where Nathan Labenz and Erik Torenberg interview AI builders and researchers; part of the Turpentine network.
quote: The Cognitive Revolution is a biweekly podcast where hosts Nathan Labenz and Erik Torenberg interview the builders on the edge of AI

claim: No Priors is a weekly podcast where Sarah Guo and Elad Gil interview leading AI engineers, researchers, and founders.
quote: Co-hosts Elad Gil and Sarah Guo talk to the world's leading AI engineers, researchers and founders about the biggest questions regarding AI's impact.

claim: The TWIML AI Podcast hosted by Sam Charrington brings ML/AI ideas to a community of researchers, data scientists, and engineers; recent 2026 episodes cover diffusion LLMs, multi-agent systems at Capital One, and reasoning in small LMs.
quote: Recent episodes include topics like 'How Capital One Delivers Multi-Agent Systems,' 'The Race to Production-Grade Diffusion LLMs,' … 'AI Trends 2026: OpenClaw Agents, Reasoning LLMs, and More'

claim: Latent Space is becoming a podcast network in 2026: more shows, more hosts, an AI for Science companion show, and integration with the AINews daily brief (80k subs).
quote: The 1-2 hour interview podcast format is cool but Alessio and I can only do so much … new shows, including one focused on AI for Science with different hosts … integrating AINews (which has 80,000 subscribers)

claim: Dwarkesh's April 2026 interview with Jensen Huang covered TPU competition, US chip controls on China, NVIDIA's supply-chain moat, and why NVIDIA isn't a hyperscaler.
quote: Jensen Huang – TPU competition, why we should sell chips to China, & Nvidia's supply chain moat

claim: Nathan Lambert is a senior research scientist and post-training lead at Ai2; he leads work on Olmo open models and is known for RewardBench and the Open LLM Leaderboard.
quote: Nathan Lambert is a senior research scientist and post-training lead at the Allen Institute for AI (Ai2), where he trains open language models (Olmos) … prominent research works in post-training language models, including Olmo, Tülu, RewardBench

claim: Lex Fridman episode #490 (Feb 2026) 'State of AI in 2026' with Nathan Lambert and Sebastian Raschka covered pre/mid/post-training, AGI timelines, and AI compute clusters.
quote: #490 – State of AI in 2026: LLMs, Coding, Scaling Laws, China, Agents, GPUs, AGI

claim: ChinaTalk is the Jordan Schneider podcast and newsletter on China, technology, and US-China relations.
quote: Jordan Schneider is the founder of the ChinaTalk Podcast and Newsletter.

claim: ChinaTalk grew its substack 60% to 65k subscribers in 2025 with growing focus on China AI lab/policy/application coverage and a weekly defense show.
quote: the substack grew 60% to 65k subscribers, with a growing focus on defense, including the launch of a weekly Second Breakfast show

claim: Ben Thompson's Stratechery Plus bundle includes Stratechery Update, Stratechery Interviews, Sharp Tech, and Dithering (the 15-minute show with John Gruber); all four are active in April 2026.
quote: Sharp Tech remains active … 'OpenAI's Enterprise Pivot, The Rise of Agents and Bubble Counterpoints' … Dithering … 'Jensen Huang's Jobsian Keynote' (April 14) … Stratechery Interviews continue, with recent interviews including discussions with F1 driver Nico Rosberg (April 16)

claim: Software Engineering Daily releases ~3 episodes/week with 2,100+ episodes since 2015; covers cloud, AI, developer culture, and runs a monthly SED News recap with Gregor Vand and Sean Falconer.
quote: The podcast is active and releases 3 episodes per week … 2,100 episodes available … a monthly podcast called 'SED News' where hosts Gregor Vand and Sean Falconer unpack the biggest stories

claim: Software Engineering Radio is run by IEEE Computer Society / IEEE Software, focusing on tutorial-style episodes; 2026 episodes include Martin Kleppmann on local-first software (Apr 15) and Marc Brooker on spec-driven AI dev (Mar 4).
quote: Software Engineering Radio is brought to you by the IEEE Computer Society and IEEE Software magazine. Since 2006 … SE Radio 716: Martin Kleppmann on Local-First Software from April 15, 2026, and SE Radio 710: Marc Brooker on Spec-Driven AI Dev from March 4, 2026.

claim: Lenny's Podcast is the product/career/growth interview show by Lenny Rachitsky; recent 2026 AI guests include Boris Cherny (Claude Code lead at Anthropic) and Simon Willison.
quote: Boris Cherny, creator and head of Claude Code at Anthropic, appeared on the podcast to discuss how Claude Code grew from a quick hack to 4% of public GitHub commits

claim: Simon Willison's April 2026 Lenny's Podcast appearance argued November 2025 was the inflection where AI coding agents crossed from 'mostly works' to 'actually works'; he now writes 95% of his code from his phone.
quote: Simon Willison discussed how November 2025 was a key inflection point when AI coding agents crossed from 'mostly works' to 'actually works,' and how he now writes 95% of his code from his phone.

claim: BG2 is Brad Gerstner (Altimeter) and Bill Gurley's biweekly markets/AI/tech podcast; 2026 episodes featured Jensen Huang on the OpenAI $100B partnership and OpenAI's Nick Turley on ChatGPT-as-super-assistant.
quote: BG2Pod is an open-source bi-weekly conversation with Bill Gurley and Brad Gerstner … An episode with Jensen Huang … discussing the new era of AI, including a $100B partnership with OpenAI

claim: Sharp Tech with Ben Thompson and Andrew Sharp covers tech analysis aligned with Stratechery's frame; recent 2026 topics include TSMC pricing power, Meta CapEx, and OpenAI's enterprise pivot.
quote: Recent episodes in 2026 have covered topics including Meta earnings and Meta's CapEx plans, TSMC's pricing power in the AI era, and OpenAI's pivot to enterprise, agents, and Nvidia's inference pivot.

claim: Jerod Santo retired from The Changelog in March 2026 after 13 years and 1042 podcast episodes; Adam Stacoviak continues running the show.
quote: After 13 years, 1042 podcasts, 452 newsletters, and countless friends made along the way, Jerod Santo said goodbye to The Changelog … He shipped his final News episode on Monday and Adam shipped their Friends finale the following day.
